QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_8

question.text: Section 60 of the Income Tax Act deals with which of the following situations?

answer.text: Income of a minor child clubbed with parents
Transfer of income without transfer of the asset — income is clubbed with the transferor
Set-off of losses from one head against income from another head
Carry forward of business losses beyond 8 years

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 60 specifically addresses situations where a person transfers the right to income from an asset without transferring the asset itself. In such cases, the income continues to be taxable in the hands of the transferor. For example, if Mr. A owns a property but directs the rental income to Mr. B without transferring the property, the rent is still taxable in A's hands. **The Trap:** Section 64 (not 60) deals with minor child income clubbing. Candidates often confuse Sections 60-64 which all deal with different aspects of clubbing. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers Section 60 at a conceptual level for understanding anti-avoidance provisions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Warn clients who try to redirect investment income (like dividends or rent) to family members without transferring the underlying asset — Section 60 nullifies this strategy.
